name: agent_tool
summary: Spawn a sub-agent to handle a focused subtask.

Returns the sub-agent's final output (foreground) or its agent id (background). The sub-agent inherits the caller's provider, model, working directory, event handler, and cancel signal; tools and prompts come from the registered template named in `agent`.
constraints: Brief the sub-agent like a smart colleague who just walked into the room — it has not seen this conversation, does not know what you have tried, does not know why this matters.
Explain what you are trying to accomplish and why; describe what you have already learned or ruled out; give enough context that it can make judgment calls.

Use **foreground** (default) when you need the result before proceeding. Use **background** when you have independent work to do in parallel; the result is delivered to your work inbox when it completes.
anti_patterns: To read a specific file — use `read_file_tool`.
For any task a single tool call can accomplish.
cautions: IMPORTANT: never delegate understanding. Do not write "based on your findings, do the task." Write prompts that prove you understood the problem and what specifically needs to happen.
IMPORTANT: when `agent` is a registered name, fields like `identity` are ignored — the registered template's role wins.
output: Plain text (foreground): the sub-agent's final response — typically free text, or the validated JSON when `contract` is set.
Plain text (background): a single line of the form `spawned <agent_id>`. The eventual output arrives later as a peer message in the caller's inbox; this tool does not block waiting for it.
On failure: an error message describing why the spawn could not start (unknown `agent` name, missing runtime context, schema-invalid `contract`).

description.description: Short label for the spawned agent, shown in events (3-8 words).

task.description: The full instruction for the sub-agent: goal, context, constraints, success criteria.

agent.description: Name of a registered sub-agent template. Omit for an ad-hoc agent.

identity.description: Role prompt for an ad-hoc agent. Ignored when `agent` is set. Defaults to a generic helper identity.

model.description: Override the model for this spawn (default: inherit from caller).

max_steps.description: Cap the agentic loop for this spawn (ad-hoc default: 10; registered templates inherit their own cap).

max_request_tokens.description: Cap output tokens per LLM request (wire field: `max_tokens`).

max_input_tokens.description: Cap cumulative input tokens across the whole spawn.

max_output_tokens.description: Cap cumulative output tokens across the whole spawn.

max_contract_retries.description: Override structured-output retry count for this spawn.

contract.description: JSON Schema (object) the sub-agent's final reply must conform to. The validated JSON is returned as this tool's result.

max_request_retries.description: Override transient-API retry count for this spawn.

request_retry_delay.description: Override base delay (ms) for request retries.

background.description: Run in background (default: false). Returns immediately with an agent id; the completed output is posted to the caller's work inbox.
